title: Statuspage Documentation
prompt_source: Inline docs prompt; CONNECTOR_DOCUMENTATION_GUIDELINES fetched via getDocGuidelines()

sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
**Atlassian Statuspage** is the incident-communications SaaS used by ops teams to publish service-health updates to their customers. This connector — `StatuspageConnector` (`CONNECTOR_TYPE = "statuspage"`, `AUTH_TYPE = "api_key"`) — wraps the operational surfaces a Shielva tenant typically needs from a Statuspage page:

| Surface | Base path | Capability |
|---|---|---|
| Pages | `/pages` | List + read pages under the token |
| Components | `/pages/{id}/components` | List, get, create, patch, delete components |
| Component Groups | `/pages/{id}/component-groups` | List grouped components |
| Incidents | `/pages/{id}/incidents` | List, get, create, patch incidents |
| Maintenances | `/pages/{id}/incidents/scheduled` | List scheduled maintenance windows |
| Subscribers | `/pages/{id}/subscribers` | List, create, delete email/SMS subscribers |
| Metrics | `/pages/{id}/metrics` | List metrics configured on the page |
| Incident Templates | `/pages/{id}/incident_templates` | List incident templates |

The connector normalises incidents + maintenances into `NormalizedDocument` (id = `f"{tenant_id}_{source_id}"`), surfaces standalone `async def` methods per user-requested operation (OCP), retries 429/5xx with exponential backoff + jitter (3 attempts), and never embeds raw HTTP in `connector.py` (SOC — all HTTP delegated to `client/http_client.py::StatuspageHTTPClient`).

sections.title: Quick Start
sections.content:
### 1. Generate a Statuspage API token

1. Sign in to https://manage.statuspage.io.
2. Open **User Profile → API Tokens**.
3. Click **Create API Token**, name it (e.g. `shielva-prod`), and copy the value — Statuspage shows it only once.
4. From **Pages → your page**, copy the **Page ID** out of the admin URL: `https://manage.statuspage.io/pages/<PAGE_ID>`.

### 2. Install in ACP

In **ACP → Manage Connectors → Statuspage**, fill in:

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| `api_key` (secret) | Token from step 3 |
| `page_id` (text) | Page ID from step 4 |
| `base_url` (text, optional) | Override `https://api.statuspage.io/v1` (rarely needed) |
| `rate_limit_per_min` (number) | Soft cap, default 30 |

Click **Install**. `install()` probes `GET /pages/{page_id}` to verify the token + page binding, then persists the config and returns `ConnectorStatus(HEALTHY, CONNECTED)`.

### 3. First health check

The gateway calls `health_check()`, which re-runs the same probe. On 2xx → `HEALTHY + CONNECTED`. On 401 → `DEGRADED + INVALID_CREDENTIALS`. On 404 (wrong page_id) → `DEGRADED + INVALID_CREDENTIALS`. On 429 → `DEGRADED + CONNECTED` with a structured-log warning.

sections.title: Authentication
sections.content:
Statuspage uses **server-to-server API token authentication** (`AUTH_TYPE = "api_key"`). No OAuth dance, no token refresh, no expiry.

### ⚠️ Statuspage gotcha — `OAuth` not `Bearer`

The published auth header uses the literal keyword **`OAuth`**, not `Bearer`. Sending `Authorization: Bearer <token>` is silently rejected with a 401.

```
Authorization: OAuth <api_key>          ← literal 'OAuth' prefix
Content-Type:  application/json
Accept:        application/json
```

The HTTP client enforces the `OAuth ` prefix; the unit test `test_install_auth_error_uses_oauth_header` asserts it explicitly.

### Lifecycle

| Phase | Behaviour |
|---|---|
| `install()` | Probes `GET /pages/{page_id}` (or `/pages` if no page_id). Validates token + page binding. |
| `authorize(...)` | Returns a `TokenInfo` whose `access_token = api_key` and `token_type = "OAuth"` — there is no exchange. |
| `health_check()` | Same `GET /pages/{page_id}` probe — cheapest reachable endpoint. |
| `ensure_token()` | Not called — no token to refresh. |
| token storage | The API token is passed through to `set_token` for parity with OAuth connectors. |

sections.title: Configuration
sections.content:
### Install fields (per-tenant)

Read via `self.config.get(...)` in `connector.py::__init__` — never hardcoded.

| Key | Type | Required | Read in code | Purpose |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `api_key` | secret | yes | `self.api_key` | `Authorization: OAuth <api_key>` header value |
| `page_id` | string | yes | `self.page_id` | Default page UUID scoping every call (overrideable per-method) |
| `base_url` | string | no | `self.base_url` (defaults to `https://api.statuspage.io/v1`) | Override for sandbox / proxy |
| `rate_limit_per_min` | number | no | `self.rate_limit_per_min` (default 30) | Client-side soft cap |

### Class constants (provider-wide)

```python
REQUIRED_CONFIG_KEYS = ["api_key", "page_id"]
_STATUS_MAP = {
    401: ("OFFLINE",   "TOKEN_EXPIRED"),
    403: ("UNHEALTHY", "INVALID_CREDENTIALS"),
    429: ("DEGRADED",  "CONNECTED"),
}
_DEFAULT_BASE_URL = "https://api.statuspage.io/v1"
```

sections.title: API Methods
sections.content: All public async methods on `StatuspageConnector`. SOC: `connector.py` orchestrates only; HTTP lives in `client/http_client.py::StatuspageHTTPClient`.

children.title: Lifecycle
children.content:
### `async install() -> ConnectorStatus`
Validate `api_key`; probe `GET /pages/{page_id}` (or `/pages` if no page_id). Returns `HEALTHY + CONNECTED` on 2xx; classifies 401/403/404 via `_STATUS_MAP`.

### `async authorize(auth_code, state) -> TokenInfo`
No-op for api_key — returns a `TokenInfo` wrapping the configured token (`token_type="OAuth"`).

### `async health_check() -> ConnectorStatus`
Probes `GET /pages/{page_id}` (the same call as `install()`).

### `async sync(since=None, full=False, kb_id=None, webhook_url=None) -> SyncResult`
Iterates Statuspage incidents + scheduled maintenances → `NormalizedDocument` → `ingest_document`. Returns `SyncResult` with `documents_synced / _failed / _found`.

children.title: Pages
children.content:
### `async list_pages(*, page=1, per_page=100) -> List`
`GET /pages?page={N}&per_page={M}`.

### `async get_page(page_id="") -> Dict`
`GET /pages/{page_id}` — falls back to `self.page_id` when arg is empty.

children.title: Components
children.content:
### `async list_components(page_id="") -> List`
`GET /pages/{page_id}/components`.

### `async get_component(page_id, component_id) -> Dict`
`GET /pages/{pid}/components/{cid}`.

### `async create_component(page_id, name, description=None, status='operational', group_id=None, showcase=True, only_show_if_degraded=False) -> Dict`
`POST /pages/{id}/components` — body: `{"component": {...}}`.

### `async update_component(page_id, component_id, fields) -> Dict`
`PATCH /pages/{pid}/components/{cid}` — body: `{"component": {...fields}}`.

### `async update_component_status(page_id, component_id, status) -> Dict`
Convenience for the common case: `{"component": {"status": status}}`.

### `async delete_component(page_id, component_id) -> Dict`
`DELETE /pages/{pid}/components/{cid}`.

### `async list_component_groups(page_id="") -> List`
`GET /pages/{id}/component-groups`.

children.title: Incidents & Maintenances
children.content:
### `async list_incidents(page_id, *, q=None, limit=100, page=1) -> List`
`GET /pages/{id}/incidents` — optional substring search via `q`.

### `async get_incident(page_id, incident_id) -> Dict`
`GET /pages/{pid}/incidents/{iid}`.

### `async create_incident(page_id, name, *, status='investigating', impact_override=None, body='', component_ids=None, components=None, deliver_notifications=True) -> Dict`
`POST /pages/{id}/incidents` — body: `{"incident": {...}}`. `components` is an `{component_id: new_status}` map Statuspage will apply atomically when the incident is created.

### `async update_incident(page_id, incident_id, fields) -> Dict`
`PATCH /pages/{pid}/incidents/{iid}` — body: `{"incident": {...fields}}`.

### `async list_maintenances(page_id, *, limit=100, page=1) -> List`
`GET /pages/{id}/incidents/scheduled` — list scheduled maintenance windows.

children.title: Subscribers, Metrics, Templates
children.content:
### `async list_subscribers(page_id, *, type=None, state=None, limit=100, page=1) -> List`
`GET /pages/{id}/subscribers` — filterable by `type` (email/sms/webhook/slack) and `state` (active/unconfirmed/quarantined).

### `async create_subscriber(page_id, *, email=None, phone_number=None, phone_country=None, page_access_user=None) -> Dict`
`POST /pages/{id}/subscribers` — body: `{"subscriber": {...}}`. Pass `email` for email subs, `phone_number`+`phone_country` for SMS, `page_access_user` for restricted-page audience subs.

### `async delete_subscriber(page_id, subscriber_id) -> Dict`
`DELETE /pages/{id}/subscribers/{sid}`.

### `async list_metrics(page_id="") -> List`
`GET /pages/{id}/metrics`.

### `async list_incident_templates(page_id="") -> List`
`GET /pages/{id}/incident_templates`.

sections.title: Error Handling
sections.content:
Exception hierarchy in `exceptions.py`:

```
StatuspageError                       # base; carries status_code + response_body
├── StatuspageAuthError               # 401 / 403
├── StatuspageBadRequestError         # 400
├── StatuspageNotFoundError           # 404
├── StatuspageConflictError           # 409
├── StatuspageRateLimitError          # 429 — retry_after_s
└── StatuspageServerError             # 5xx
```

Back-compat aliases preserved:
```
StatuspageNetworkError = StatuspageServerError    # legacy name
StatuspageNotFound      = StatuspageNotFoundError
```

### Retry behaviour (`client/http_client.py::_request`)

| Status | Action |
|---|---|
| 400 / 401 / 403 / 404 / 409 | Raise immediately |
| 429 | Honour `Retry-After`; otherwise exponential backoff `_BASE_DELAY * 2 ** attempt` (0.5s → 1s → 2s) + jitter, up to `_MAX_RETRIES=3` |
| 5xx | Same exponential backoff, retry up to 3 |
| `httpx.TimeoutException` / `httpx.TransportError` | Backoff, retry up to 3, then raise `StatuspageNetworkError` |

### How errors surface to the gateway

`health_check()` maps via `_STATUS_MAP`:

```
401 → ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED,  INVALID_CREDENTIALS)
403 → ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED,  INVALID_CREDENTIALS)
404 → ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED,  INVALID_CREDENTIALS)   — page_id wrong
429 → ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED,  CONNECTED)             — logged as warning
5xx → ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED,  CONNECTED)             — provider outage
```

sections.title: Troubleshooting
sections.content:
| Symptom | Probable cause | Fix |
|---|---|---|
| `ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE, MISSING_CREDENTIALS)` at install | `api_key` blank | Re-enter the token in ACP. Statuspage shows the token only once at creation — rotate if lost. |
| `ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED, INVALID_CREDENTIALS)` w/ 'not found' in message | `page_id` doesn't match a page the token can see | Copy the page UUID from `https://manage.statuspage.io/pages/<PAGE_ID>` and re-install. |
| `ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED, INVALID_CREDENTIALS)` at health_check | 401 — token revoked or sent as `Bearer` | Confirm the HTTP client sends `Authorization: OAuth …`. Rotate the token in **User Profile → API Tokens** if needed. |
| `statuspage.http.retry` warnings spike | Hitting 429 (Statuspage per-token rate cap, ~1 req/sec) | The client retries with `Retry-After`-aware exponential backoff. Lower `rate_limit_per_min` or batch fewer requests. |
| `ValueError: page_id is required` at runtime | Method called with empty `page_id` arg and no `page_id` install_field configured | Set `page_id` at install time, or pass `page_id="…"` to each call. |
| `ImportError: cannot import name 'StatuspageNetworkError'` | Older `exceptions.py` without back-compat aliases | Already fixed — `StatuspageNetworkError = StatuspageServerError` alias added. |
| Component `create_component` returns 422 | `status` value not in Statuspage's allowed enum | Use one of `operational`, `under_maintenance`, `degraded_performance`, `partial_outage`, `major_outage`. |
| Subscriber `create_subscriber` returns 400 | None of `email`, `phone_number`, or `page_access_user` supplied | Pass at least one identifier; for SMS, both `phone_number` AND `phone_country` are required. |
